summary: Synced Melee's project scaffolding with newer dtk-template conventions: bumped decomp-toolkit/objdiff/sjiswrap/wibo/Nix inputs, rewrote large parts of tools/project.py for objdiff report-based progress and compile_commands generation, added regression/change reporting helpers, refreshed VSCode settings/tasks, and made a few source/config fixes required by the newer tooling. The PR mattered mostly as infrastructure: it moved progress tracking from the older project-local progress.json calculation to objdiff-cli report data, made clangd/editor workflows more automatic, and resolved several tool-update fallout items such as SJIS/comment encoding issues, manual asm/object label handling, and a troublesome grtpichu/grtpikachu data label boundary.
what_changed: Bumped dtk-template-related tool versions in configure.py and Nix: decomp-toolkit from v1.0.0 to v1.5.1, objdiff from v2.2.0 to v2.7.1, sjiswrap from v1.1.1 to v1.2.1, and wibo to 0.6.16.
Added a Nix derivation for objdiff 2.7.1 and wired it into .nix/default.nix and .nix/melee.nix as objdiff-cli; removed the custom .nix/devkitppc.nix package from the overlay/build inputs.

Reworked tools/project.py heavily: added typed build-config dictionaries, compiler/wrapper helpers, file type helpers, link_order_callback support, scratch_preset_id metadata, objdiff report args, optional progress toggle, compile_commands.json generation, and objdiff-report-backed progress printing.

Changed progress generation from a local progress.json byte/object calculation to objdiff-cli report generate producing build/<version>/report.json; calculate_progress now reads that report and prints matched/linked code/data/function/unit measures.
Added baseline/change-regression Ninja targets: baseline, changes, changes_all, and regressions.md using objdiff-cli report changes plus the new tools/changes_fmt.py formatter.
Added tools/changes_fmt.py to summarize objdiff report changes as plaintext or markdown, separating regressions from progressions and diffing unit/function percent keys.

Updated decompctx.py to take include directories through -I/--include, emit dependency data from a module-level deps list, support #pragma once in addition to include guards, and pass include paths from MWCC flags.
Updated download_tool.py so download/extract logic is factored into download(), and added a fallback for CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED using certifi/ssl when available.
Removed tools/upload_progress.py, indicating the old frogress upload path was no longer part of this template sync.

Generated compile_commands.json by translating a subset of MWCC flags into clang/clangd-compatible arguments, including include/define rewriting and options for exceptions, RTTI, language, enum size, and common BSS.

Refreshed VSCode recommendations and settings: removed replacerules configuration, recommended clangd plus Python black/flake8, disabled Microsoft C/C++ IntelliSense, added a default ninja build task, and associated .inc/.clangd files.
Expanded .gitignore for objdiff.json, compile_commands.json, decompctx outputs, build/cache/IDE folders, original DOL/REL/map artifacts, and macOS/Python artifacts.

Adjusted GALE01 split order by moving Runtime/__mem.c later near MetroTRK/mem_TRK.c; the PR body showed this update was tied to broken OSInit/OSInterrupt/OSSync functions after the template/tool update.
Adjusted GALE01 symbols around 0x803E9264-0x803E9270: @89 gained align:4, grTPk_803E9270 was added as an object, and ...data.0 became a local label instead of an object.
Cleaned non-ASCII text in comments/header labels: Olifr� became Olifre, Poké/Pokémon became Poke/POKEMON/Pokemon, consistent with the PR body's mention of SJIS encoding errors.
Made minor formatting-only cleanups in tools/fix.py and tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py.
smart_moves: Preserved existing objdiff symbol_mappings when regenerating objdiff.json, avoiding loss of manual objdiff work during template regeneration.

Separated report generation from progress printing: Ninja creates report.json through objdiff-cli, while configure.py progress mode only reads and formats it. This makes progress semantics follow objdiff instead of a custom byte-count implementation.
Added regression infrastructure directly to generated Ninja targets, so future PRs can create a baseline and compare later report.json output without writing ad hoc scripts.
Generated compile_commands.json from the same object configuration used for MWCC builds, reducing editor drift and giving clangd source paths/includes/defines that track decomp configuration.

Inserted missing -lang=c or -lang=c++ flags per source when neither base nor extra cflags specify language, and did it on a per-object copy of extra_cflags to avoid mutating shared flag lists unintentionally.
Filtered include-directory flags out of objdiff scratch c_flags while still using those include flags for decompctx and compile_commands, keeping objdiff/decomp.me scratch metadata cleaner.
Made decompctx include directories explicit through -I flags generated from object cflags, which is more robust than hardcoding include/ only.
Added #pragma once handling in decompctx, useful for modernized headers that may not use only traditional include guards.
Introduced config.link_order_callback with an example for non-matching builds, providing a controlled hook for add/remove/reorder experiments without hand-editing generated dtk config.
Used order_only Ninja dependencies for pre-compile/post-compile/post-link/post-build phony steps, clarifying sequencing without forcing unnecessary rebuilds from timestamps.

Kept the PR checklist visible in the body and resolved concrete fallout items before merge: Nix version bumps, manual asm label breakage, SJIS encoding errors, and the lbl_803E926F/grtpichu data issue.
decomp_lessons: Toolchain/template updates can break matching indirectly through generated config, symbol metadata, split ordering, or encoding; verify with objdiff/report output and inspect any 100% to 0% regressions before assuming source code changed.
When updating dtk/objdiff, preserve manual objdiff symbol mappings across objdiff.json regeneration; otherwise regenerated config can destroy local matching aids.

Prefer ASCII in comments and public headers when tools may process files as Shift-JIS or through Windows wrappers; even comments like accented names and Poké/Pokémon text can become practical build/tooling problems.

For ambiguous adjacent data, explicit symbol metadata may be needed: adding align:4 to @89 and declaring grTPk_803E9270 as an object while demoting ...data.0 to a label addressed the noted lbl_803E926F/grtpichu issue.
Progress should be driven by the same diffing engine used to verify matching. This PR moved progress from custom progress.json calculations to objdiff report measures, reducing duplicated logic.
clangd support in a decomp repo should be generated from project configuration, not maintained manually; convert only the MWCC flags clangd can understand and ignore/rewrite the rest.
Custom build steps are safer as named phony stages with order-only relationships; this keeps asset/tool steps ordered without turning every timestamp change into a full relink.
When source files can be C, C++, or asm, centralize suffix checks with helpers like file_is_c/file_is_cpp/file_is_asm to avoid inconsistent extension handling.
A template sync is a good moment to remove obsolete project-specific services; here the old frogress upload helper was deleted as objdiff report output became the progress source.
Nix reproducibility updates must track both version strings and hashes for dtk, objdiff, sjiswrap, wibo, and nixpkgs; partial bumps were explicitly called out as a PR checklist item.
naming_conventions: Build/version defines were modernized from version/VERSION to BUILD_VERSION and VERSION_<config.version> in configure.py, e.g. --defsym BUILD_VERSION=... and -DVERSION_GALE01-style defines depending on config.version.
New ProjectConfig fields follow descriptive snake_case names: generate_compile_commands, extra_clang_flags, scratch_preset_id, link_order_callback, print_progress_categories, progress_report_args.
Progress/change report Ninja targets use short searchable names: progress, report, baseline, changes, changes_all, regressions.md, and always.
Generated objdiff unit names are normalized to forward-slash paths with module_name/base_object rather than platform-specific separators.
The source/header text cleanup preferred ASCII spellings in comments: Poke/Pokemon/POKEMON instead of Poké/Pokémon, and Olifre instead of Olifr�.
The symbol fix kept local numeric labels like @89 but added explicit metadata such as align:4 and introduced grTPk_803E9270 for the object at 0x803E9270.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved Runtime/__mem.c in config/GALE01/splits.txt; the PR body showed broken manual/runtime labels in OSInit, OSInterrupt, and OSSync after the update, so split/link ordering was part of the matching repair.
Changed symbol metadata near .data:0x803E9264-0x803E9270 to handle a label boundary: @89 size 0xB gained align:4, grTPk_803E9270 was added as an object, and ...data.0 became a label.

Added a link_order_callback hook that can reorder or insert objects for non-matching builds while leaving matching builds untouched; this is useful for controlled experiments without polluting normal link order.
objdiff scratch generation now maps MW compiler versions including GC/2.0p1 and records reverse_fn_order when -inline deferred/nodeferred flags imply reversed function order.
objdiff report changes are used as regression checks; report_changes compares a baseline report to the current report and changes_fmt surfaces percent drops at total, unit, and function granularity.

The PR author noted an objdiff CLI/GUI discrepancy for HSD_SynthSFXBankDeflagSync: CLI reported 100.0% -> 75.0 while the GUI showed 100%. The evidence says it was ignored pending future resolution, not fixed in this PR.
review_feedback: No formal review comments were present in the slice.

The PR body contained a completed checklist: Nix versions needed bumping; manual asm labels were broken in objdiff; SJIS encoding errors needed investigation; lbl_803E926F should be part of grtpichu.o @89.
The PR body included an initial failure block reporting functions broken from 100% to 0 in main/dolphin/os/OSInit, OSInterrupt, and OSSync.

A later issue comment said objdiff CLI complained that main/sysdolphin/baselib/synth HSD_SynthSFXBankDeflagSync went from 100.0% to 75.0, but the author planned to ignore it because the objdiff GUI still showed 100% matching.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching objdiff CLI progress discrepancies or HSD_SynthSFXBankDeflagSync after objdiff v2.7.1.
Search for future changes to config/GALE01/symbols.txt around @89, grTPk_803E9270, or lbl_803E926F to see whether the label-boundary fix held.
Look for later PRs modifying tools/project.py progress_report_args, report changes, or compile_commands generation after this template sync.
Search for remaining non-ASCII comments or Poké/Pokémon text if SJIS/tool encoding errors recur.
Check subsequent PRs for removal or adoption of link_order_callback in actual non-matching build workflows.
